title: A2 Part-year tax-free threshold 2019
description: Complete question A2 if you were not an Australian resident for the whole financial year.
keywords: TaxPack, part-year threshold, tax-free threshold, tax-free, A2, 2019, paper tax return, paper form, paper return, paper lodgement

content_blocks.text: If you were not an Australian resident for the whole 2018–19 year, we use the information you show at this item on your tax return to work out your tax-free threshold.

items: Work out your residency status for tax purposes

content_blocks.text: In 2018–19 did you become an Australian resident, or stop being an Australian resident?

rows: Go to question A3 Government super contributions 2019 .

content_blocks.text: Did you receive any Australian Government pension or allowance, which you showed at item 5 or item 6?

rows: Go to Completing your tax return .

content_blocks.text: Was this amount only for youth allowance, Newstart Allowance, a sickness allowance, a special benefit, or a payment under the Community Development Employment Projects (CDEP) Scheme?

rows: Go to Completing your tax return .
You are entitled to the full tax-free threshold. You do not need to answer this question. Go to question A3 Government super contributions 2019 .

content_blocks.text: Write the date you became, or stopped being, an Australian resident for tax purposes in the Date box at item A2 .
Write the number of months that you were an Australian resident in 2018–19 (counting the first month during which you became, or stopped being, a resident) at N item A2 .

items: became a resident in November 2018 and remained a resident for the rest of the income year, you would write 8
stopped being a resident in September 2018, you would write 3.

content_text:
If you were not an Australian resident for the whole 2018–19 year, we use the information you show at this item on your tax return to work out your tax-free threshold.
See also:
- Work out your residency status for tax purposes
In 2018–19 did you become an Australian resident, or stop being an Australian resident?
No | Go to question A3 Government super contributions 2019 .
Yes | Read on.
Did you receive any Australian Government pension or allowance, which you showed at item 5 or item 6?
No | Go to Completing your tax return .
Yes | Read on.
Was this amount only for youth allowance, Newstart Allowance, a sickness allowance, a special benefit, or a payment under the Community Development Employment Projects (CDEP) Scheme?
Yes | Go to Completing your tax return .
No | You are entitled to the full tax-free threshold. You do not need to answer this question. Go to question A3 Government super contributions 2019 .
## Completing your tax return
### Step 1
Write the date you became, or stopped being, an Australian resident for tax purposes in the Date box at item A2 .
### Step 2
Write the number of months that you were an Australian resident in 2018–19 (counting the first month during which you became, or stopped being, a resident) at N item A2 .
Example
If you:
- became a resident in November 2018 and remained a resident for the rest of the income year, you would write 8
- stopped being a resident in September 2018, you would write 3.
We will work out your tax-free threshold for you using the information you provide at this item. If you want to work it out, go to:
- Tax-free threshold for newcomers to Australia
- Tax-free threshold if you are leaving Australia with the intention to reside overseas .
## Where to go next
- Go to question A3 Government super contributions 2019 .
- Return to main menu Individual tax return instructions 2019 .
- Go back to question A1 Under 18 2019 .
